citation: Brunsgaard et al. (2011) - Evaluation of the Indoor Environment of Comfort Houses: Qualitative and Quantitative Approaches
description: Section-skeleton few-shot example for design strategy extraction. Blue-highlighted passages from the annotated PDF are preserved as relevant_snippets.
section_skeleton:
Paper structure (Brunsgaard et al., 2011 — 20 pages, ~76,000 characters):
--- TITLE & ABSTRACT (page 1) ---
[...about evaluation of indoor environment in Comfort Houses, the first certified passive houses in Denmark...]
--- INTRODUCTION (pages 1-2) ---
[...background on energy consumption in buildings, passive houses, Comfort House project — describes the buildings and design approach...]
"The results of a case study on some of the first certified passive houses in Denmark, called Comfort Houses, are presented in this paper."
"A passive house is usually very well insulated, making it draught-free; the majority of its windows are orientated towards the sun; and it has a ventilation system with very efficient heat recovery."
--- METHODS (pages 2-5) --- ← LOOK HERE
"The indoor environment in the Comfort Houses is evaluated using both quantitative measurements and qualitative interviews"
[...mixed methods, quantitative measurements, qualitative interviews...]
--- RESULTS (pages 5-15) ---
[...indoor temperature (summer/winter), indoor air quality (CO2, RH), daylight factors, acoustics — THESE ARE ECOSYSTEM SERVICES NOT DESIGN STRATEGIES]
--- DISCUSSION (pages 15-18) ---
[...analysis of occupant behaviour, system performance — IGNORE THIS]
--- CONCLUSIONS (pages 18-19) ---
[...summary of findings — IGNORE THIS]

relevant_snippets.text: The results of a case study on some of the first certified passive houses in Denmark, called Comfort Houses, are presented in this paper
relevant_snippets.why_relevant: Identifies the design strategy: comfort houses as a type of low-energy passive house

relevant_snippets.text: A passive house is usually very well insulated, making it draught-free; the majority of its windows are orientated towards the sun; and it has a ventilation system with very efficient heat recovery.
relevant_snippets.why_relevant: Describes the core implementation details of the passive house design: window orientation, ventilation system, heat recovery

relevant_snippets.text: the majority of its windows are orientated towards the sun
relevant_snippets.why_relevant: Implementation detail: window orientation towards the sun for passive solar gains

relevant_snippets.text: it has a ventilation system with very efficient heat recovery
relevant_snippets.why_relevant: Implementation detail: ventilation system with heat recovery

relevant_snippets.text: the houses are heated using ventilated air and the temperature is centrally controlled in the ventilation unit and not in each room
relevant_snippets.why_relevant: Implementation detail: the heating system relies on ventilated air with central temperature control

design_strategies.name: Comfort houses (low-energy passive houses)
design_strategies.anchor: first certified passive houses in Denmark, called Comfort Houses
implementation_details: majority of its windows are orientated towards the sun
ventilation system with very efficient heat recovery
heated using ventilated air and the temperature is centrally controlled

lesson: From this 20-page paper, the design strategy is a broad approach (comfort/passive house design) rather than a single technology. The implementation details are the specific features that make up this design: window orientation for solar gains, a mechanical ventilation system with heat recovery, and a centralized heating system using ventilated air. The model should look for the overarching design strategy and its key components. Results sections describe ecosystem services (thermal comfort, air quality, daylight, acoustics) NOT design strategies.
